summary: npm package abused as jsDelivr-fronted browser redirector to external site
details: px8my ships a browser-only main file (px.js) that, when loaded via cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/px8my/px.js, injects a fullscreen iframe pointing at a hardcoded external URL (https://mfmz.yltvip99.cn/H.html?c=0gjr), redirecting the viewer off-site. The tarball also includes update_px8my.sh, a maintenance script that rotates the hardcoded redirect domain, bumps the patch version, runs `npm publish`, and calls purge.jsdelivr.net to flush the CDN cache so the new domain is served immediately. A comment in that script notes it uses Python urllib rather than curl to evade the `tirith` scanner. The package is not a functional Node library; the npm registry is being used as a free, mutable distribution channel for a browser redirect payload fronted by jsDelivr. Any site or developer that includes this package via jsDelivr (or bundles it) will forcibly redirect end-users to an attacker-controlled destination.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
